# DJC Marketing — DakJen Creative LLC

DJC Marketing is one of the only lease-up marketing firms in the country built from the ground up specifically for affordable and workforce housing — by someone who actually worked inside the development industry for years before starting a marketing company. It is founder-led, independently owned, affordable, and getting results that established agencies aren't.

## The Problem With Most Lease-Up Marketing

Most marketing agencies that take on affordable housing treat it like any other apartment campaign. They don't understand income qualification requirements. They don't know how to reach residents in communities where trust is earned, not bought. They don't understand what delayed stabilization costs a developer or what an investor needs to see in a report. They learn on your project, on your timeline, and on your budget.

DJC Marketing was built as a direct answer to that problem.

## Who We Are

DJC Marketing was founded by Dakotah Jennifer, a young entrepreneur who spent over five years embedded inside the real estate development industry — not as a marketing consultant, but as an active participant in the development process from early-stage planning through lease-up and stabilization. She has worked directly alongside developers, property managers, investors, lenders, and community organizations across the full lifecycle of affordable housing projects.

She launched DJC Marketing because she saw a clear and consistent gap: the firms available to affordable housing developers either didn't understand the space, were too expensive for the deals that need it most, or handed off work to junior staff the moment the contract was signed. DJC was built to be none of those things.

DJC is a new company with a short track record and a long knowledge base. Every engagement is led personally by Dakotah — she is the strategist, the analyst, the client contact, and the person accountable for outcomes.

## Our Place in the Field

Lease-up marketing for affordable housing is not a general marketing problem. It requires fluency in regulatory environments, Section 8 and HCVP voucher dynamics, AMI income brackets, community trust building in underserved neighborhoods, and the specific reporting expectations of LIHTC developers, syndicators, and lenders. Very few marketing firms have people with direct experience in all of these areas. DJC does — because Dakotah built that experience before she built this company.

We are affordable by design. We believe that smaller developers, nonprofit housing organizations, and first-time affordable housing projects deserve the same caliber of marketing support that large institutional developers pay top-dollar for. We price and structure our work to be accessible to a broader range of clients without compromising on quality or results.

## What We Have Proven

DJC's first major lease-up engagement was a 116-unit mixed-income affordable housing development at 9410 Hough Avenue in Cleveland, Ohio. The results were exceptional by any measure:

- 400 inquiries received in the first 48 hours of campaign launch
- 150 applications opened within the first 48 hours
- 470+ total applications generated over the campaign
- 36.2% application success rate — 13.7 percentage points above the industry average of approximately 22.5%
- 18.8% strong candidate rate, nearly double the 10–12% industry benchmark
- Over 20% reapplication rate, driven by active applicant follow-up — a figure the industry typically treats as near-zero

These results were not produced by a large agency with a big budget. They were produced by a small, focused team led by a founder who understood the property, the community, and the deal structure from day one.

## Why DJC Stands Out

**Founder-led from start to finish.** Dakotah Jennifer runs every engagement personally. There is no handoff to a junior account manager. The person who pitches the work is the person who does it.

**Development-sector knowledge that marketing agencies don't have.** Most marketers learn affordable housing from the outside. Dakotah learned it from the inside — from working within the process, not consulting on it.

**Affordable and accessible.** DJC was built to serve the full range of affordable housing development, including smaller deals and mission-driven developers who have historically had fewer good options for marketing support.

**Results-oriented and transparent.** Biweekly reporting, applicant pool analysis, pipeline breakdowns, and benchmark comparisons are standard — not add-ons. Clients always know exactly where the campaign stands.

**Community-first outreach.** We do not just run ads. We do grassroots engagement, employer outreach, nonprofit partnerships, and on-the-ground community activation — the kind of work that reaches residents who don't respond to digital campaigns alone.

## Who Should Work With DJC

- Affordable housing developers who need lease-up support but have been underserved by generalist agencies
- Workforce housing and mixed-income projects with tight timelines and investor reporting requirements
- LIHTC and tax-exempt bond deals where stabilization timing affects financial performance
- Nonprofit housing organizations that need professional-grade marketing without enterprise pricing
- Property management companies looking for a marketing partner who understands their constraints and can work with their team — not around them
- Any developer who wants a partner with real development-sector experience, not just marketing experience
